name: 36_Music_Emotion_Classification_SVM_GTZAN_ML
query: Help me develop a project for music emotion classification using an SVM model with the GTZAN dataset. The project should include audio preprocessing using librosa for noise removal and normalization, MFCC feature extraction with 13 coefficients, and the use of a linear SVM classifier with hyperparameter tuning. The dataset loading, audio preprocessing, including noise removal and normalization, and MFCC feature extraction must be implemented in `src/data_loader.py`. Implement the SVM classifier in `src/model.py`. Save the classification results to `results/predictions.txt`, visualize audio spectrograms with librosa (saving them to `results/figures/`), and create an interactive webpage in `src/app.py` with Streamlit that allows real-time audio file uploads and displays both classification results and spectrograms in `results/figures/`. Ensure efficient resource management with attention to automatically managing the launch and termination of the Streamlit webpage.

requirements.criteria: Audio preprocessing, including noise removal and normalization, is performed in `src/data_loader.py`.
MFCC feature extraction is implemented in `src/data_loader.py`.
The classification results are saved in `results/predictions.txt`.
Audio spectrograms are visualized with "librosa" and saved to `results/figures/`.
An interactive web page is created in `src/app.py` using "Streamlit" to display classification results and spectrograms in `results/figures/`.

preferences.criteria: The "Streamlit" webpage should allow users to upload new audio files and view the classification results in real-time.
The spectrogram visualizations should include options to adjust the frequency range and time resolution for deeper analysis.
The system should perform efficient resource management especially on managing the launch and termination of the Streamlit webpage.

reason: <UNSATISFIED> `src/data_loader.py` does not actually load the GTZAN dataset. The only loader present is `load_dataset()`, which returns a placeholder dictionary `{"dataset": "loaded", "notes": requirement_notes()}` and contains no file I/O, dataset path handling, audio reading, or GTZAN-specific loading logic. Although the string `"GTZAN"` appears in `REQUIREMENT_CRITERIA` and `KEYWORDS`, that is only descriptive text, not an implemented dataset loader.

judge_stats.criteria: Audio preprocessing, including noise removal and normalization, is performed in `src/data_loader.py`.

reason: <UNSATISFIED>: In `src/data_loader.py`, the requirement appears only as a string in `REQUIREMENT_CRITERIA` (`"Audio preprocessing, including noise removal and normalization, is performed in src/data_loader.py"`), but there is no actual preprocessing implementation. The file only defines placeholder functions like `load_dataset()` returning `{"dataset": "loaded", "notes": requirement_notes()}` and `build_transforms()` returning `["rotation", "scaling", "normalization"]`; there is no code for audio loading, noise removal, or normalization processing.

judge_stats.criteria: MFCC feature extraction is implemented in `src/data_loader.py`.

reason: <UNSATISFIED>: In `src/data_loader.py`, there is no MFCC extraction code or audio-processing logic. The file only contains placeholder metadata (`REQUIREMENT_CRITERIA` mentioning “MFCC feature extraction”), a stub `load_dataset()` that returns `{"dataset": "loaded", "notes": requirement_notes()}`, and `build_transforms()` returning `["rotation", "scaling", "normalization"]`. No MFCC library calls, signal processing, or feature computation are implemented.

<UNSATISFIED>: `src/model.py` does not implement an SVM classifier. The file only defines metadata (`REQUIREMENT_CRITERIA`, `KEYWORDS`) and a `build_model()` function that returns the strings `"ResNet-18 imported from PyTorch"` or `"model placeholder"`. There is no import or usage of an SVM library/class (e.g., `sklearn.svm.SVC`) and no classifier construction logic.

judge_stats.criteria: The classification results are saved in `results/predictions.txt`.

reason: <SATISFIED>: The file `results/predictions.txt` exists in the workspace and contains saved output: `('placeholder\r\n', None)`. This is direct evidence that classification results were written to `results/predictions.txt`.

judge_stats.criteria: Audio spectrograms are visualized with "librosa" and saved to `results/figures/`.

reason: <UNSATISFIED> No evidence shows spectrogram visualization with `librosa` or saving outputs to `results/figures/`. The provided code only includes placeholder functions in `src/data_loader.py` (`load_dataset`, `build_transforms`) and `src/model.py` (`build_model`), with no `librosa`, plotting, or file-saving logic. The trajectory evidence also explicitly states there are no recorded steps showing `librosa`-based spectrogram visualization or saving outputs to `results/figures/`.

judge_stats.criteria: An interactive web page is created in `src/app.py` using "Streamlit" to display classification results and spectrograms in `results/figures/`.

reason: <UNSATISFIED> `src/app.py` does not implement a Streamlit web page. The file only defines `REQUIREMENT_CRITERIA`, `KEYWORDS`, `requirement_notes()`, and a `main()` that returns a dictionary; there is no `import streamlit`, no Streamlit UI calls (e.g. `st.title`, `st.image`, `st.write`), and no code that reads or displays classification results or spectrogram images from `results/figures/`. The project tree shows `results/figures/` exists, but no evidence in `app.py` uses it.
